title: ConvertKit vs Moosend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit vs Moosend. See pricing, features, and ratings to find the best email marketing platform for your needs.
h1: ConvertKit vs Moosend: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: ConvertKit and Moosend are both popular freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit focuses on creators and course sellers with premium features, while Moosend emphasizes affordability and unlimited emails for small businesses. This comparison will help you choose the right tool based on your specific needs and budget.
choose_a_if: You're a content creator, blogger, or course seller who needs built-in commerce and landing page functionality.
You prioritize visual automation workflows and advanced subscriber tagging for segmentation.
You want a platform specifically designed for the creator economy with affiliate program support.
choose_b_if: You're cost-conscious and need unlimited emails on a tight budget starting at just $9/month.
You want strong customer reviews and ratings (4.7 on G2, 4.4 on Trustpilot) from verified users.
You need A/B testing, real-time analytics, and automation workflows for a small business setup.

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ConvertKit is better for creators and course sellers who need commerce and advanced automation features, while Moosend is better for cost-conscious small businesses. Moosend actually has higher ratings on G2 (4.7 vs 4.4) and Trustpilot (4.4 vs 3.2), suggesting better overall customer satisfaction.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Moosend?
faq.a: Moosend is significantly cheaper, starting at $9/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. Both offer free tiers with 1,000 subscriber limits, but Moosend's affordable pricing makes it ideal for budget-conscious teams. ConvertKit's higher price reflects its focus on creator-specific features.

faq.q: Can I switch from Moosend to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, most email marketing platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists and migrate to another service. However, you may need to manually rebuild your automation workflows and landing pages, as these don't always transfer directly. It's recommended to plan your migration carefully to avoid losing data or email sequences.
